one_line: Imperial Chola princess and patron of temples and healing in Thanjavur and Pazhaiyarai.

appearance.age_look: Late 20s, slender yet poised, with the calm bearing of a royal strategist.
appearance.face: Oval face with soft jawline, warm medium-brown complexion, straight nose, slightly arched brows, large expressive almond eyes with kohl, a small bindi at the center of her forehead, and a composed default expression that mixes serenity with quiet authority.
appearance.hair: Long black hair parted in the middle and drawn into a single thick braid down her back, the braid wrapped at intervals with thin gold ribbons and ending in a tasselled gold-and-red hair ornament; she wears a simple narrow gold circlet resting low on the forehead just above the hairline, set with a single small red stone at the center.
appearance.costume: Era-accurate Chola royal attire: a deep maroon silk sari with a rich gold border draped in the early South Indian style (nivi-like but with the pallu brought over the left shoulder and tucked at the waist to keep her arms free), worn over a short-sleeved fitted choli blouse in muted gold; the sari’s border has small repeating leaf motifs in gold thread, and the fabric has a subtle self-pattern; at her waist a broad engraved gold kamarbandh belt anchors the pleats, with a small central pendant; she wears layered gold necklaces (a shorter choker with granulated beads and a longer manga-mala style chain), medium-sized round gold earrings, thin gold bangles stacked on both wrists, and delicate silver-toned anklets; her bare midriff and arms are visible in a dignified, non-exaggerated way, and she is barefoot or in simple leather sandals appropriate to the period.
appearance.signature: A neatly rolled palm-leaf manuscript tied with a thin red cord, held in one hand or tucked at her waist, symbolising her temple and hospital endowments, accompanied by a small bronze oil lamp she can lift in the other hand when needed.

ref_desc: Kundavai Pirati is a late-20s Chola princess with a warm medium-brown oval face, large kohl-lined eyes, long black hair in a gold-ribboned braid under a narrow gold circlet, wearing a deep maroon silk sari with gold border, layered gold jewellery and kamarbandh, and carrying a rolled palm-leaf manuscript with a small bronze oil lamp, in a palette of deep maroon, gold, muted red, earthy brown and black.
sheet_prompt: Anime-style full-body model sheet of Kundavai Pirati: a late-20s Chola princess with a warm medium-brown oval face, straight nose, slightly arched brows and large expressive kohl-lined almond eyes with a small bindi, her default expression calm and authoritative; long black hair parted in the middle, drawn into a single thick braid down her back, the braid wrapped with thin gold ribbons and ending in a tasselled gold-and-red ornament, and a narrow gold circlet with a small red stone resting low on her forehead; she wears a deep maroon silk sari with a rich gold border in early South Indian drape, pleats anchored at the front and pallu brought over the left shoulder and tucked at the waist, over a short-sleeved fitted muted-gold choli blouse, the sari’s border bearing fine gold leaf motifs and a subtle self-pattern; a broad engraved gold kamarbandh belt at her waist with a small central pendant, layered gold necklaces (one short beaded choker and one longer chain), medium round gold earrings, stacks of thin gold bangles on both wrists, delicate silver-toned anklets, and simple period-accurate sandals or bare feet on stone; her build is slender yet poised, with dignified, non-exaggerated midriff and arms; in her hands she carries a neatly rolled palm-leaf manuscript tied with a red cord and a small bronze oil lamp, and the overall colour palette is deep maroon, gold, muted red, earthy brown and black, shown from front, side and back to clearly display hair, jewellery, sari drape, belt and accessories.
